docs.title: MixMaster WECS User Manual
docs.text:
Automated Order Preparation Solutions

Manual Revision 3.0: Published February 2026

docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
Welcome to the MixMaster WECS User Manual.

  • A WECS is a Warehouse Execution & Control System.

This manual provides an in-depth understanding of the functions and features of the MixMaster WECS.

MixMaster is the central coordination platform that manages warehouse distribution, orchestrating the flow of production across multiple sub-systems including InnoPick, manual induction stations, and depalletizing terminals.

This manual is organized by the menu sections and sub-sections found in the MixMaster interface.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: How to access and log into MixMaster, including an overview of the interface.

docs.title: Home Page
docs.text: The main dashboard showing production summary, sub-system statuses, and JIT-OOS information.

docs.title: Truckloads
docs.text: Managing truckloads, pallets, groupings, and production launches.

docs.title: Replenishments
docs.text: Monitoring and managing replenishment requests for InnoPick.

docs.title: Inventory
docs.text: Viewing InnoPick inventory, flushing products, and floor position overview.

docs.title: Reports
docs.text: Automated production reports including performance, event logs, faults, and out-of-stock tracking.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Packaging format management, user accounts, operation settings, and event bus management.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: User preferences, service status, and third-party notices.

docs.title: Terminals
docs.text:
Operator terminals for Top-Off, Manual Induction, and Depalletizing tasks.

Navigation: ← Depalletizing Terminal | Getting Started →

docs.title: Replenishments
docs.text: Home > Replenishments

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Replenishments page lists all the replenishments that the system has calculated are necessary to complete the production. The page displays Active replenishments by default, but the user can select to display Completed ones also. Note the various columns that provide important information about each replenishment.

docs.title: Viewing Replenishment Details
docs.text: The user can click on the # of the replen (left-most column) and see the case sequence of the pallet whose cases made the replenishment necessary. Replenishments are only created when the system knows it will need a certain product in order to complete a client pallet. The case sequence for the pallet in question is displayed as a pop-up on the right-hand side of the screen without navigating the user away from the replenishments page.

docs.title: Viewing Product Details
docs.text:
Users can also click the SKU value and that will open up a similar pop-up with the product details.

Navigation: ← Truckload Groupings | Inventory →

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: Home > Settings

docs.title: User Settings
docs.text: The User Settings page contains options for language selection and system of units.

docs.title: Status Service
docs.text: This page contains links to various service statuses and logs that are used by developers. Visible only to those with the necessary access levels.

docs.title: Third Party Notices
docs.text:
This page contains links to various licenses for embedded systems within MixMaster. Visible only to those with the necessary access levels.

Navigation: ← Event Bus Management | Terminals →

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Home > Configuration

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Configuration section allows the authorized user to access the following pages.

docs.title: Packaging Formats
docs.text: Product properties management including editing format parameters and recovery actions.

docs.title: Product Configuration Optimizer
docs.text: Intelligent analysis of production statistics to recommend InnoPick product slotting.

docs.title: User Manager
docs.text: Creating and managing user accounts and access levels.

docs.title: Operation Settings
docs.text: Key operational parameters for Distribution, Order, and Reporting categories.

docs.title: Event Bus Management
docs.text:
Developer and system expert interface for event bus configuration.

Navigation: ← Product QC Faults | Packaging Formats →

docs.title: Event Bus Management
docs.text: Home > Configuration > Event Bus Management

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Event Bus Management interface is for use by developers and system experts only.

Navigation: ← Operation Settings | Settings →

docs.title: Operation Settings
docs.text: Home > Configuration > Operation Settings

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Operation Settings page contains many key operational parameters and options. They are divided into three categories: Distribution, Order, and Reporting. Each option and parameter has its own description. Certain settings are restricted depending on a user's access level.

Navigation: ← User Manager | Event Bus Management →

docs.title: Packaging Formats
docs.text: Home > Configuration > Packaging Formats

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Packaging Formats page displays all the configured products with their properties. This page can be used to modify these properties. For detailed explanations of each field / column, see the next section on the Product Configuration Sidebar.

docs.title: Product Configuration Sidebar
docs.text: When any Product is clicked, the configuration Sidebar opens on the right hand side of the screen and an operator with sufficient access can make changes to the product configuration.

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• SKU: Product SKU number
  • UPC: Universal Product Code for the case
  • UPC Ind. Unit: UPC for the individual sellable unit within the case (if different from the case UPC)
  • Reference Name: Reference name for the product
  • Name: Product display name
  • Category: Product category (e.g., Promotional item)
  • Un./Case: Number of individual units per case
  • Un./Layer: Number of units per pallet layer
  • Un./Pallet: Total number of units per pallet
  • Case grouping unit (Default: 1): Some products are sold as grouped units. This field is for defining how many physical cases make up one sellable unit. MixMaster then calculates distribution and palletization accordingly.
  • Product Family: This field, which is used only when the associated checkbox is enabled in the Extra Details section, is used by the Palletizer for better handling of the cases in the Row-Builder.
  • Retired: Whether the product has been retired from production
  • Do not sub.: Whether the product should not be substituted with another product that shares its UPC
  • Need superior wrap program: Whether the product requires a special wrap program (double wrapping). If any case with this option enabled are found on a pallet, the entire pallet will be wrapped with the special wrap program.

docs.title: InnoPick Configurations
docs.text:
• InnoPick Compatible: Whether the product can be assigned to InnoPick
  • Allow replenishments: Whether the system can call depalletizing replenishments for this product
  • Use Manual Induction: Whether the product must be assigned to Manual Induction instead of Depalletizers

docs.title: Dimensions
docs.text:
• Length / Width / Height: Case dimensions in mm
  • Layer length / Layer width: Layer dimensions in mm
  • Gross Weight: Case weight in grams
  • Volume: Case volume in ml
  • Case Dimension / Weight and Layer Dimension Tolerance: Allowable variance in dimensions (+/- mm or g). If set to 0, default tolerances will be used.
  • Box Top Width / Box Top Length: Top face dimensions in mm
  • Height For Robots: Height value used by robotic systems in mm, if a different height than the product height is needed. If set to 0, the product height is used.

docs.title: Extra Details
docs.text:
Explanations:

  • Force 4 layers per replenishment (replenishment quantity may use a second lane): Used for high-movers to force larger replenishments, with the understanding that this can cause a replenishment to fill up more than one InnoPick lane.
  • Keep close to descrambler (used for high-volume or difficult-to-clamp products): When enabled, MixMaster will request this product to be kept close to the descrambler deposit station for better efficiency when depalletizing.
  • Use product family field for palletizer instructions instead of dimension-based algorithm: When enabled, MixMaster will use the Product Family designation from the General Section. This is used by the Palletizer for better handling of the cases in the Row-Builder.

docs.title: Editing Packaging Format Properties
docs.text: • Note: In order to edit packaging configurations, the user simply has to click Edit to make the various parameter fields editable. The user can also check the Lock box to prevent MixMaster from overwriting the user-set parameters with other values.

docs.title: Send product details to InnoPick
docs.text:
• This recovery action makes MixMaster send product info for this SKU to InnoPick.
    • MixMaster normally sends all product info to InnoPick at the start of every truckload launch, so this recovery action is only needed if there is a new product that the operators want to configure in a specific way inside InnoPick Manager.
    • With this feature, the product could be sent to InnoPick Manager and then the user could configure the product to be forced to a certain level or have a certain minimum replenishment size, or some other specific configuration.

docs.title: Query encompass inventory
docs.text: • This queries Encompass inventory values for the given SKU. Example:

docs.title: Request replenishment to destination
docs.text:
• This option allows the user to manually request a replenishment for the product to be created either for one of the Depals or for Manual Induction:

Navigation: ← Configuration | Product Configuration Optimizer →

docs.title: Product Configuration Optimizer
docs.text: Home > Configuration > Product Configuration Optimizer

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Product Configuration Optimizer is a feature that intelligently analyzes the past or upcoming production statistics to make recommendations on which products should be slotted in InnoPick.

docs.title: Optimizer Mode
docs.text:
The Mode drop-down menu is where the user can select which production statistics to optimize the system on.

The Optimizer results can help users and production supervisors to improve the efficiency of InnoPick, especially during periods when products are being introduced or removed, or when the volume profile changes significantly.

Navigation: ← Packaging Formats | User Manager →

docs.title: User Manager
docs.text: Home > Configuration > User Manager

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The User Manager page allows Admin-level users to create and manage User accounts.

docs.title: Creating a New User
docs.text:
When creating a new user account, the following information is required.

Each user is given a Role, which can be one of the following options: Admin, Manager, Clerk, or Terminal User. Each has different levels of access:

  • Admin: Full access to all controls and options.

  • Manager: Access to all operational settings, but not site settings.

  • Clerk: Access necessary to operate the system for production, but not change settings.

  • Terminal User: Restricted login to Manual Induction station or Depal screens.

Navigation: ← Product Configuration Optimizer | Operation Settings →

docs.title: Daily Operations
docs.text: Home > Daily Operations

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This section covers the standard procedures for operating MixMaster on a daily basis, including startup, launching production, monitoring, and end-of-day procedures.

docs.title: Startup
docs.text: Complete checklist for beginning operations each day, including system verification, truckload preparation, and production launch.

docs.title: Pre-Production Checklist
docs.text:
Before launching production, verify the following:

System Readiness:

  • MixMaster is accessible in the browser and you are logged in with the appropriate access level
  • All sub-systems (InnoPick levels) are ready to go
  • No critical priority alerts are displayed in the top banner
  • Verify that there are no lingering Truckloads or Replenishments from previous production days that were not properly completed.

Truckload Readiness:

  • Truckload data has been received from the client system and is visible on the Truckloads page
  • Truckloads are in Available status
  • Verify the delivery dates and truckload details match the production schedule

Inventory and Replenishment Readiness:

  • Confirm that Depalletizing stations are staffed and ready to process replenishments
  • If Manual Induction is being used, confirm the station is staffed

docs.title: Launching Production
docs.text:
Once the pre-production checklist is complete:

  1. Navigate to the Truckloads page

  2. Create or verify Truckload Groupings according to the production schedule and priorities

  3. Select the first group and to open the Launching and Resetting interface on the right-hand side of the screen

    • Note: To access the Launch option, you must click on the Truckload or Group Heading:

  1. Click Launch

  1. Verify the truckloads change status from Available to In Queue

  2. Confirm that replenishment requests begin appearing on the Replenishments page

  3. Monitor the first few minutes of production to verify cases are being assigned to sub-systems correctly

Important:

  • The first group of truckloads will be completed before the second group. Plan groupings accordingly.
  • MixMaster may rearrange pallet order within a group to optimize production across sub-systems.
  • If a truckload was launched by mistake and no pallets have started production, use Safely Reset to revert it to Available status.

docs.title: Startup After Extended Downtime or System Updates
docs.text:
If the system has been down for several days or there have been system updates performed, extra caution is recommended:

  1. Verify all sub-systems are online and communicating (check Sub-System Status)
  2. Check for any truckloads left in In Progress or In Queue status from before the downtime
  3. Coordinate with supervision about whether to resume, reset, or delete incomplete truckloads
  4. Launch a small test group first to verify end-to-end flow before committing to full production

docs.title: Monitoring Production
docs.text: Ongoing monitoring tasks, recognizing normal and abnormal operation, and responding to issues during production.

docs.title: End of Day
docs.text:
End-of-shift procedures, shift handoff, and overnight considerations.

Navigation: ← Loading MixMaster | Monitoring Production →

docs.title: End of Day
docs.text: Home > Daily Operations > End of Day

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Proper end-of-day procedures ensure that production is accounted for, the system is left in a known state, and the next shift has the information they need.

docs.title: Verify Production Status
docs.text:
Before ending the shift, check the following:

  1. Truckload Status

    • Navigate to the Truckloads page
    • Verify that all launched truckloads have reached Completed status
    • If any truckloads are still In Progress, coordinate with supervision about whether to wait for completion or hand off to the next shift

  2. Replenishment Status

    • Check the Replenishments page
    • Verify that active replenishments have been completed
    • Note any pending replenishments that will carry over to the next shift

  3. Production Summary

    • Review the Production Summary for final counts
    • Confirm completed numbers match expectations

docs.title: Review Reports
docs.text:
At the end of production, review the following reports for any issues that should be documented or escalated:

  • Faults History: Note any recurring faults during the shift
  • Out of Stock: Review products marked OOS — these may need replenishment before the next production run
  • Automation Performance: Review production performance and note any items or events that need to be followed up on.

docs.title: Log Out
docs.text:
1. Click on your username or logout option
  2. Confirm logout
  3. Close the browser

Security Note: Always log out when leaving your workstation to prevent unauthorized access.

Navigation: ← Monitoring Production | Home Page →

docs.title: Monitoring Production
docs.text: Home > Daily Operations > Monitoring Production

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: During production, operators should actively monitor MixMaster to ensure smooth flow across all sub-systems, catch problems early, and maintain production efficiency.

docs.title: Home Page
docs.text:
The Home Page provides the highest-level view of the current state of production.

  1. Production Summary

    • Track completed vs. total truckloads, pallets, and cases
    • Monitor the contribution of each sub-system (InnoPick, Manual)

  2. Sub-System Status

    • All InnoPick levels should show Automatic (green) during production
    • Idle indicates a level is not actively processing
    • Faulted requires immediate attention

  3. JIT-OOS Visuals

    • Watch for Just-In-Time Out-Of-Stock events, which pause production while the system recalculates
    • Monitor priority alerts in the top banner

docs.title: Truckloads Page
docs.text:
The Truckloads page provides the most detailed operational monitoring.

  1. System Overview metrics (top of page)

    • L1–L5 replenishment counts: These numbers should be steadily decreasing as replenishments are completed. If they are not decreasing, investigate.
    • Inventory Snapshot: Monitor case counts per level. A level dropping to very low inventory may need attention.
    • Available Lanes / Disabled Lanes: A high number of disabled lanes reduces system capacity.

  2. Truckload progress

    • Watch for truckloads advancing from In Queue → In Progress → Completed
    • If truckloads are stalling in In Queue or In Progress, investigate sub-system status and replenishment flow.

docs.title: Replenishments Page
docs.text:
The Replenishments page shows whether the sub-systems are being adequately supplied.

  • Active replenishments should be progressing through their lifecycle
  • Watch for replenishments that remain in a pending state for too long
  • Depalletizing and Manual Induction stations need to be actively processing replenishments to keep production moving

docs.title: Monitoring Schedule
docs.text:
Every 15–30 Minutes:

  • Check the Home Page for sub-system statuses and priority alerts
  • Verify production progress is advancing (Production Summary numbers increasing)

Every 1–2 Hours:

  • Review replenishment status — are replenishments completing at a healthy rate?
  • Check the System Overview on the Truckloads page for inventory levels and lane availability
  • Verify truckloads are progressing as expected

Throughout the Shift:

  • Watch for JIT-OOS events, which indicate out-of-stock products are disrupting production
  • Coordinate with Depal and Manual Induction operators to ensure replenishments are being processed
  • Be aware of downstream palletizer status — if downstream backs up, it can affect MixMaster flow

docs.title: Recognizing Normal Operation
docs.text:
When the system is operating correctly:

  • All sub-system statuses show Automatic
  • Production Summary numbers are steadily increasing
  • Replenishments are flowing through their lifecycle without stalling
  • Truckloads are advancing from In Queue through In Progress to Completed
  • No priority alerts in the top banner
  • Replenishment queue counts (L1–L5) are decreasing as production progresses

docs.title: Warning Signs Requiring Immediate Attention
docs.text:
• Any sub-system showing Faulted status
  • Priority alerts appearing in the top banner
  • A JIT-OOS event firing (production pauses while the system recalculates)
  • Truckloads stuck in In Progress with no cases completing

docs.title: Warning Signs to Monitor Closely
docs.text:
• Replenishment counts (L1–L5) not decreasing — may indicate Depal or Manual Induction bottleneck
  • Inventory levels on one or more InnoPick levels dropping significantly lower than others
  • Multiple disabled lanes on a single level
  • Truckloads taking longer than expected to complete
  • Repeated OOS cases appearing for the same product

docs.title: Responding to Issues
docs.text:
When an issue is detected:

  1. Identify the scope

    • Is it affecting one sub-system/level or the entire system?
    • Is production stopped or just slowed?

  2. Check the priority alerts in the top banner for details

  3. Check the Faults History report for recent fault information from MixMaster and sub-systems

  4. Take appropriate action

    • For sub-system faults: Coordinate with InnoPick operators or maintenance to resolve the fault on the affected level
    • For truckload issues: Use the Truckload Controls to manage affected pallets (Hold, Reset, etc.)
    • For replenishment bottlenecks: Coordinate with Depal and Manual Induction operators
    • For JIT-OOS events: Wait for the system to finish recalculating, then verify production resumes
    • For unknown issues: Contact supervisor or DRL remote support

  5. Log the event using the Event Logs report for follow-up and historical tracking

Navigation: ← Daily Operations | End of Day →

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Home > Getting Started

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This section covers the basics of accessing and logging into the MixMaster Warehouse Execution & Control System.

MixMaster is a web-hosted application accessed through a browser.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: An overview of MixMaster WECS and how this manual is organized.

docs.title: Loading MixMaster
docs.text:
How to open MixMaster in a browser, log in, and access the various terminals.

Navigation: ← Home | Introduction →

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: Home > Getting Started > Introduction

docs.title: About MixMaster WECS
docs.text:
Welcome to the MixMaster WECS User Manual, designed to provide you with an in-depth understanding of the function and features of MixMaster Warehouse Execution & Control System.

This manual has been prepared to guide users of all levels through the use and configuration options of the software.

The Manual is generally organized by the Menu Section and Sub-Sections found in the MixMaster interface.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Proceed to Loading MixMaster to learn how to access the system.

Navigation: ← Getting Started | Loading MixMaster →

docs.title: Loading MixMaster
docs.text: Home > Getting Started > Loading MixMaster

docs.title: Accessing MixMaster
docs.text:
The first step is to open a browser and navigate to the web-hosted MixMaster address.

Once the landing page has loaded, the user must Login. The login block on the left is used to access the main MixMaster interface. On the right, the user can see the additional Terminals that can be accessed. These terminals are set up for operators for specific tasks.

docs.title: Logging In
docs.text:
Enter a Username and Password into the window prompt and click Sign In. Alternatively, one can sign in as a guest. Guest users do not have access to a number of things: configuration settings, recovery actions, and other features that can affect production.

  • After logging on, the user will remain logged in until the session is closed or the user logs off.

  • Depending on the level of access granted to the user, some menus may not be accessible.

  • For details about the Top-Off, Manual Induction, and Depalletizer Terminals, see Terminals.

Navigation: ← Introduction | Home Page →

docs.title: Home Page
docs.text: Home > Home Page

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Home Page gives the user a high-level summary of the current state of production and various system statuses.

docs.title: Production Summary
docs.text: Total production volume and completion status across sub-systems.

docs.title: Sub-System Status
docs.text: Status indicators for each InnoPick level: Faulted, Idle, or Automatic.

docs.title: JIT-OOS Visuals
docs.text:
Just-In-Time Out-Of-Stock information and priority alerts.

Navigation: ← Loading MixMaster | Production Summary →

docs.title: JIT-OOS Visuals
docs.text: Home > Home Page > JIT-OOS Visuals

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
On the right side of the Home Page, there is information related to Just-In-Time-Out-Of-Stock (JIT OOS) processes.

A JIT OOS occurs when a group of pallets has already started being produced and one of the SKUs is found to be Out of Stock. The system then pauses all production while it recalculates the pallets affected by the missing SKU in order to minimize Top Off.

docs.title: Priority Alerts
docs.text:
The Home Page, along with all the other pages, displays priority alerts in the right corner of the top banner.

  • This can be clicked on and expanded:

The operator has two buttons he can press:

  • View the History of Alerts.
  • Clear All the Alerts

Navigation: ← Sub-System Status | Truckloads →

docs.title: Production Summary
docs.text: Home > Home Page > Production Summary

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The first leftmost section of the Home Page is the Production Summary. Here one can see what the total production volume is, and how much of it has been completed in terms of pallets or cases.

docs.title: Reading the Production Summary
docs.text:
• Under the General heading, the various sub-systems which together make up the entire distribution system are listed, with the contribution of each indicated. At Suncoast, this includes InnoPick and Manual.

  • The Completed numbers only include the Truckloads, pallets, and pieces that are finalized according to MixMaster. Anything that is still In Progress will not be counted.

Navigation: ← Home Page | Sub-System Status →

docs.title: Sub-System Status
docs.text: Home > Home Page > Sub-System Status

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The next block of content on the Home Page is the Sub-System Status indicator. At Suncoast the sub-systems are the five levels of InnoPick. The different statuses are: Faulted, Idle, and Automatic.

Navigation: ← Production Summary | JIT-OOS Visuals →

docs.title: Inventory
docs.text: Home > Inventory

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Inventory page provides the user with an overview of all the cases currently in the system (as seen by MixMaster).

docs.title: InnoPick Inventory
docs.text: Active products and case counts by level and lane.

docs.title: Flush
docs.text: Manually emptying InnoPick of certain products.

docs.title: Floor Positions
docs.text:
Overview of depalletizing floor positions and their current status.

Navigation: ← Replenishments | InnoPick Inventory →

docs.title: Floor Positions
docs.text: Home > Inventory > Floor Positions

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Floor Positions page provides an overview of the depalletizing floor positions and their current status.

Navigation: ← Flush | Reports →

docs.title: Flush
docs.text: Home > Inventory > Flush

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Flush feature is used to manually empty InnoPick of certain products. This can be useful when trying to free up space in InnoPick, or to empty out expired products, or limited-time promotional products that are no longer valid to be sold. This feature does create a special Truckload so it is not recommended to use it in the middle of production.

docs.title: Confirming a Flush
docs.text: When a product is selected to be Flushed, a confirmation pop-up will appear.

docs.title: Flush Truckload
docs.text:
If the Flush operation is confirmed, a custom Truckload is created and launched; and InnoPick receives the case sequence from MixMaster. The Flush Truckload will be visible in the Truckloads page:

Navigation: ← InnoPick Inventory | Floor Positions →

docs.title: InnoPick Inventory
docs.text: Home > Inventory > InnoPick Inventory

docs.title: Products View
docs.text: This page lists all the active products for which there are cases in InnoPick. The Refresh button queries InnoPick Manager to get an updated snapshot of the current inventory.

docs.title: Lanes View
docs.text:
The Lanes tab lists the same inventory in terms of the specific InnoPick lanes (by level and lane number).

Navigation: ← Inventory | Flush →

docs.title: Reports
docs.text: Home > Reports

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Reports Page consists of automatically-produced reports of various types.

docs.title: Automation Performance
docs.text: Production metrics summarized on a horizontal time scale.

docs.title: Event Logs
docs.text: User and operator-generated events for issue identification and follow-up.

docs.title: InnoPick State
docs.text: Input, output, and state summaries for the levels of InnoPick.

docs.title: Faults History
docs.text: All faults reported to MixMaster from InnoPick and other sub-systems.

docs.title: Out of Stock
docs.text: Cases marked Out of Stock during production.

docs.title: Product QC Faults
docs.text:
Cases rejected at QC stations.

Navigation: ← Floor Positions | Automation Performance →

docs.title: Automation Performance
docs.text: Home > Reports > Automation Performance

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This report is produced for every production date and summarizes various production metrics on a horizontal time scale.

Navigation: ← Reports | Event Logs →

docs.title: Event Logs
docs.text: Home > Reports > Event Logs

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This report is a list of user and operator-generated events. This is a tool provided for issue identification and follow-up. The page lists previously-created events, which can be filtered by status (Pending, In Progress, or Closed), and new events can be created with the + Add event log button.

docs.title: Creating a New Event
docs.text:
When creating a new event, the user can input all the relevant information, including time, system zone (level and lane if applicable), symptoms, causes, and comments.

Navigation: ← Automation Performance | InnoPick State →

docs.title: Faults History
docs.text: Home > Reports > Faults History

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This report lists all the faults that have been reported to MixMaster. MixMaster has its own faults that it generates, but it also receives fault information from InnoPick and possibly other sub-systems. The faults can be filtered and searched.

Navigation: ← InnoPick State | Out of Stock →

docs.title: InnoPick State
docs.text: Home > Reports > InnoPick State

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The InnoPick state report summarizes the input, output, and various states of the levels of InnoPick across a horizontal timescale. Note that the numbers are mostly minutes.

Navigation: ← Event Logs | Faults History →

docs.title: Out of Stock
docs.text: Home > Reports > Out of Stock

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Out of Stock report lists all of the cases that were marked Out of Stock by MixMaster for the selected time period. These cases were in the Truckloads before launch but during the launch sequence (and perhaps later in the production process, or manually marked as such by the user) these products were not available and so were marked as Out of Stock and the client pallet was produced without them. The report can be downloaded as a .csv file.

Navigation: ← Faults History | Product QC Faults →

docs.title: Product QC Faults
docs.text: Home > Reports > Product QC Faults

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This report lists all the cases that were rejected at the QC stations, along with the relevant data that is available for them.

Navigation: ← Out of Stock | Configuration →

docs.title: Terminals
docs.text: Home > Terminals

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: There are three different types of user terminals: Top-Off, Manual Induction, and Depalletizing.

docs.title: Logging onto a Terminal
docs.text: When the users select one of the terminals, a number pad appears on the right. A valid number is required. Once the number is entered, click on the large Access terminal button.

docs.title: Top-Off Terminal
docs.text: Terminal used by operators working at the Top-Off station.

docs.title: Manual Induction
docs.text: Manually inducting cases to InnoPick, including replenishment-based and non-replenishment induction.

docs.title: Depalletizing Terminal
docs.text:
Managing depalletizing tasks, confirming pallets, and depositing layers.

Navigation: ← Settings | Top-Off Terminal →

docs.title: Depalletizing Terminal
docs.text: Home > Terminals > Depalletizing Terminal

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: When an operator is logged onto a Depalletizer Terminal, the page will display the various pallets (with their product details) that are currently in the Depal Floor Positions, as well as the number of Available Tasks that the operator can complete.

docs.title: Starting a Task
docs.text:
To confirm and begin a task, the operator has to click on the Start Next button on the top right of the screen. When a task is begun by the operator, a pop-up window appears in the center of the page and the operator must either scan the pallet position or enter it in manually, to confirm that the correct pallet is in view.

After the pallet position is confirmed, a display appears with the particulars of the depalletizing task.

docs.title: Confirming Layers
docs.text:
The operator then removes layers and presses the blue button, Confirm layer placed, after each layer deposit. This button will make another pop-up appear; the operator must press the OK button for the deposited layer of product to move downstream.

Navigation: ← Manual Induction | Home (Beginning of Manual)

docs.title: Manual Induction
docs.text: Home > Terminals > Manual Induction

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Manual Induction Terminal is used to manually induct cases to InnoPick. This can be used for replenishments (if the product is configured to be inducted manually), or inducting other cases that are not specifically associated with production.

docs.title: Replenishment Manual Induction
docs.text: When there is an active replenishment due for the Manual Induction station, the Replenishment request induction button on the left-hand side will be highlighted in yellow.

docs.title: Confirming the Replenishment
docs.text: When the user clicks on the button above, a pop-up window will appear indicating what the replenishment details are. The operator must confirm the pallet position (or scan it) to make sure the correct pallet is being depalletized:

docs.title: Completing the Replenishment
docs.text:
Once the replenishment has been confirmed, the user must complete it by scanning / entering the cases one by one. When a replenishment is active, the screen displays it at the top, shows the product details and # of cases to scan in the middle, and also totals for the number of cases "inducted in this session" at the bottom.

The operator then scans a case or enters the UPC code into the dialog box at the top.

The interface will indicate that the current replenishment is actively being inducted by highlighting the product in the middle of the screen.

Back on the Replenishments page, the active replenishment will show the Status as "In Progress" and will show visually how much of the full amount has been inducted.

The operator then must induct the requested number of cases, or press Short Remaining Cases to end the replenishment early.

docs.title: Inducting Cases Without a Replenishment
docs.text: It is also possible to induct loose cases at the Manual Induction station which are not part of a replenishment. To do this, the user must click on the button Return Customer cases without pallet HU while no other replenishment is active. Once in this mode, the operator can scan or manually enter the UPC code for the cases being inducted.

docs.title: Confirming the Product
docs.text:
Unlike with the replenishment-based induction, in this mode the operator can induct cases of multiple types.

Navigation: ← Top-Off Terminal | Depalletizing Terminal →

docs.title: Top-Off Terminal
docs.text: Home > Terminals > Top-Off Terminal

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Top-Off Terminal is used by the operators working at the Top-Off station.

Navigation: ← Terminals | Manual Induction →

docs.title: Truckloads
docs.text: Home > Truckloads

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Truckloads Page gathers all the information related to the production Truckloads. They are divided by status: Available, In Progress, and Completed.

docs.title: Buttons to Filter by Status
docs.text:
The buttons with the 3 statuses allow the user to filter the displayed Truckloads according to their current status.

Each Truckload is summarized in one horizontal line, and the status of each Truckload is indicated by the color of that line.

  • If the Truckload is Available, it is White
  • If In Progress, it is Green
  • If Completed, it is Grey.

docs.title: System Overview
docs.text:
At the top of the Truckloads page, as well as at the top of the Replenishments page, there is a summary of various system metrics.

  • The L1 to L5 numbers on the left indicate the number of active Replenishments due for each Level of InnoPick. These replenishments are queued per level. As production first begins, pallets are put into progress and replenishment requests are processed and queued. As the number of replenishments decreases (as seen in the numbers above), more pallets are automatically set into progress and more replenishments are requested.

  • Inventory Snapshot: These numbers indicate the number of cases on each level of InnoPick, as well as the Total Cs. InnoPick number.

  • InnoPick Lanes Summary: The user can also see at a glance how many lanes are empty (Available Lanes) and how many lanes are disabled (Disabled Lanes).
  • Click on the numbers to get a level-by-level breakdown of the Available and Disabled lanes.

docs.title: Truckload List
docs.text: Viewing, expanding, and inspecting individual truckloads and their pallets.

docs.title: Truckload Controls
docs.text: Managing pallets and truckloads: Complete, Hold, Reset, Delete, and other actions.

docs.title: Truckload Groupings
docs.text:
Grouping truckloads for production launches and managing group operations.

Navigation: ← JIT-OOS Visuals | Truckload List →

docs.title: Truckload Controls
docs.text: Home > Truckloads > Truckload Controls

docs.title: Pallet-Level Controls
docs.text: When a truckload is selected there are various functions that can be executed via the Manage drop-down menu. These commands affect selected individual pallets inside the Truckload.

docs.title: Complete Pallet
docs.text: This will change the status of the selected pallet(s) to Completed. It should not be used outside of specific support scenarios, as directed by DRL.

docs.title: Hold
docs.text:
• This function will prevent any cases from this pallet from being scheduled for output on any sub-system.
  • If this is enabled, a pop-up appears on the Truckloads page of MixMaster on the top right:

docs.title: Clear Hold
docs.text: This function removes a Hold that has been placed, allowing InnoPick (or other sub-system) to schedule output cases for that Pallet.

docs.title: Force Assign Palletizer
docs.text:
• This feature allows an operator to resend the palletizing instructions to a specific palletizer.
  • This is a recovery tool to be used only when needed, and with care.

docs.title: Delete Pallet
docs.text:
• This will remove the selected pallet(s) from the Truckload
  • This should NOT be used by system operators
  • It is a function that is sometimes used by system experts when troubleshooting problems

docs.title: Truckload-Level Controls
docs.text: Additional Controls are available when navigating the Truckloads list. These commands affect the selected truckload(s).

docs.title: Logs
docs.text: • These are logs from MixMaster used by system experts for specific troubleshooting scenarios.

docs.title: Refresh
docs.text: • This will update any statuses that are not reflecting the latest updates, usually referring to products that have been enabled or disabled with regard to a Pending truckload.

docs.title: Safely Reset and Back to Available
docs.text:
• This can be executed only for truckloads that have been launched (In Queue or In Progres)

  • It will reset all the pallets for which no cases have already been scheduled and set the truckload back to Available.

  • If some of the pallets have already started being scheduled for output, then those pallets will remain In Progress, resulting in a partially-reset Truckload.

  • If a Truckload has been partially reset through this function, it can be safely put back to In Progress because it will only produce those pallets which were completely reset (and therefore not produced).

  • Note: If a Truckload is reset, that does not cancel the replenishment pallets which were requested to the Depalletization area.

docs.title: Reset
docs.text:
• This feature forces a Reset of the Truckload and should only be used as part of a remote support effort guided by system experts.
  • Warning: Resetting pallets during production is likely to cause irreversible changes and problematic disconnects between the various interconnected systems.

docs.title: Delete
docs.text:
• This action will delete the selected Truckload(s) from the page.
  • Like the Reset function above, this action should only be used as part of a remote support effort guided by system experts.
  • Warning: Deleting pallets during production is likely to cause irreversible changes and problematic disconnects between the various interconnected systems.

Navigation: ← Truckload List | Truckload Groupings →

docs.title: Truckload Groupings
docs.text: Home > Truckloads > Truckload Groupings

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Users can group the truckloads according to the particulars of their production priorities and schedule. Each group can then be "Launched" or put into production. This action changes the status of the truckloads in question, calculates how their production is going to be accomplished, and assigns all the cases to their respective sub-systems.

docs.title: Viewing Truckload Groups
docs.text:
See the screenshots below for a view of the Truckload Groups when they are collapsed, and when they are expanded.

Truckload Groups Collapsed:

Truckload Groups Expanded:

Within a group of truckloads, MixMaster may rearrange the pallet order to balance and optimize production between the various sub-systems. But the first group of truckloads will be completed before the second group begins.

docs.title: Launching and Resetting Groups
docs.text: Truckloads are launched using the Launching and Resetting interface that appears on the right-hand side of the Truckloads page when a group is selected. MixMaster considers the launched truckloads that haven't actually started being built as In Queue. While they are in this state they can easily be reset using the Safely Reset button, which reverts them from In Queue back to Available.

docs.title: Managing Groups
docs.text:
It's also possible to Edit the group's name, Delete the grouping (without deleting the Truckloads), or Delete the Grouping AND the Truckloads.

Navigation: ← Truckload Controls | Replenishments →

docs.title: Truckload List
docs.text: Home > Truckloads > Truckload List

docs.title: Viewing Truckloads
docs.text: When MixMaster receives Truckload information from the client's system, it displays all available Truckloads on the page. Each truckload is made up of a certain number of client pallets.

docs.title: For Groups / Truckloads:
docs.text:
• Truckload: Truckload # or Name
  • Equipment: Associated Equipment Details
  • TruckNumber: Number of the associated Truck
  • Destination
  • Delivery Date: Date when the Group is scheduled for delivery to client.
  • Pallets: # of Pallets within the Group / Truckloads
  • InnoPick: # of cases from InnoPick for this Group / Truckload
  • TopOff: # of cases going to TopOff for this Group / Truckload
  • OOS: # of cases that are Out of Stock for this Group / Truckload
  • Status: Pending, In Queue, Progress Bar (showing level of copmletion).

docs.title: Expanding Truckload Details
docs.text: A truckload can be selected and expanded by clicking the magnifying glass icon. This will make a pop-up appear with the Truckload details.

docs.title: For Pallets within Groups / Truckloads:
docs.text:
• Pallet: Pallet ID
  • Pallet Type: 1 (32x37) or 2 (40x48)
  • Palletizer: PAL_A or PAL_B (only visible once it has been assigned)
  • InnoPick: # of cases from InnoPick for this Pallet
  • TopOff: # of cases going to TopOff for this Pallet
  • OOS: # of cases that are Out of Stock for this Pallet
  • Sequence: The build order of the Pallets for this Truckload
  • Status: Pending, In Queue, In Progress, or Completed.

docs.title: Expanding Pallet Details within Group / Truckloads
docs.text:
Each horizontal line represents a different pallet within the Truckload. And each pallet can be expanded to view its products by clicking the + sign:

Alternatively, the user can click Expand All to see the details of all the Truckloads in that Group:

docs.title: For Pallets within Truckloads:
docs.text:
Note: The expanded details show a list of products within the Pallet in the order of ascending SKU #s. This is not the case sequence used for palletizing. For that, one must click on the View Case Sequence button (see next sub-section).

  • SKU: The SKU # of this / these cases
  • Product: Product description
  • InnoPick: # of these cases coming from InnoPick on this pallet
  • TopOff: # of these cases going to TopOff on this pallet
  • OOS: # of these cases marked as Out of Stock on this pallet

docs.title: Viewing the Case Sequence
docs.text:
When a pallet is selected, the user can click View Case Sequence to see more case details (if it has been launched), including the exact sequence of cases that MixMaster calculated in order to build it.

Note:

  • Only available for pallets that have been launched.
  • Some details become visible as production of the pallet progresses, such as Origin HU, Date Code, Palletizer, etc.

docs.title: For Cases within a Pallet's Case Sequence:
docs.text:
• ID: The unique case ID within this pallet's sequence
  • SKU: The product SKU number (clickable — opens the Product Details pop-up)
  • Name: Product description
  • Origin HU: The Handling Unit (pallet) from which this case originated
  • Date Code: The date code associated with the case
  • Sequence: The position of this case within the pallet build, shown as Layer-Position (e.g., 1-0, 1-1, 2-0)
  • Source: The sub-system responsible for this case (e.g., InnoPick)
  • Level: The Level of InnoPick that will supply the case
  • Spiral: The Spiral or Merge that the case (and the whole pallet) is assigned to
  • Palletizer: The palletizer the pallet is assigned to
  • Sequence #: The global sequence number for this case across all production

docs.title: Viewing Product Details
docs.text: In the Truckloads page, it is also possible to click any of the Product SKU numbers to open a pop-up and see more details on that specific product. For more detailed information on the Product Packaging Formats, see Packaging Formats.

docs.title: Recovery Actions
docs.text:
• See Packaging Formats > Recovery Actions for details on available recovery actions.

Navigation: ← Truckloads | Truckload Controls →
